_description: Empirical hospitality benchmarks gathered from public sources 2026-04-18. Anchors for calibration of sensation-tracker thresholds, review probabilities, and segment expectations.

derived_segment_expectations._method: Extrapolated from brand tier mapping — confidence B

ultra_luxury_aman_fs_ritz_gran_melia.note: Properties like Four Seasons, Aman sit 55-75 empirically

tripadvisor_participation_rate.source: Marchiori & Cantoni (2015) + Dinis et al. (2019), analyzed in Springer ENTER 2019 book
tripadvisor_participation_rate.interpretation: Very low — this is the CROSS-platform rate of guests who write on TripAdvisor specifically

by_traveler_segment_estimated._derivation: Triangulated from MARA + industry panel data + stay-behavior correlations (Cornell CHR 2023)

review_writing_delay_days_by_segment._source: Composite of Booking.com post-stay CTA conversion data + TripAdvisor unprompted submission timing analysis

platform_share_of_reviews_written_europe._description: Given a hotel guest writes a review, which platform do they choose?
platform_share_of_reviews_written_europe.source: Phocuswright European Consumer 2024 + internal cross-platform analysis

cancellation_rates_empirical._source: Cornell CHR 2023 + Hotel Booking Demand Kaggle dataset n=119K

peak_end_rule_correction_rates._interpretation: Negativity bias well-documented: bad experiences cool slower than good. Checkout moment matters disproportionately (peak-end). Positive values = warming (less extreme), negative = cooling.

limits_and_caveats: All industry averages have confidence intervals ±5-10pp depending on sample
Segment-level probabilities are triangulated, not directly sampled per-archetype
Regional variation (EU vs US vs APAC) is significant — a single global avg can mislead
Post-COVID behavior shifted review propensity up ~15-20pp industry-wide; pre-2020 studies under-report current rates
